reasoning: The Lean conjunction captures both existence and uniqueness of the Sylow p-subgroup of H equal to P ∩ H. The first conjunct (∀ R, R.toSubgroup = (H ⊓ P).subgroupOf H) gives uniqueness, and together with Nonempty (Sylow p H) it forces (H ∩ P).subgroupOf H to actually be a Sylow p-subgroup of H (by taking any witness R and using the equation). The encoding (H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H correctly realizes "P ∩ H viewed as a subgroup of H," matching Sylow p H. The Fintype G hypothesis aligns with the finite-group context of Dummit-Foote Chapter 4. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped, and the two directions of the equivalence to (a ∧ b) both hold.
